definitions: An impolite way of telling someone to go away or to express contempt for something.
To reject something forcefully and rudely.
examples: When he offered me that lowball price, I told him to stick it where the sun don't shine.
She told her boss to stick his unreasonable demands where the sun don't shine.
lemma: stick it where the sun don't shine
source: stick it where the sun don't shine
